item.title: MSCI Says Low Free-Float Among Many Hurdles for Vietnam Stocks
item.description: MSCI Inc. pointed to low free-float levels at some firms and continued limits on foreign ownership as among hurdles for Vietnam stocks, potentially complicating the country’s push for a market upgrade.

item.title: Traders’ Latest AI-Related Play Is a Struggling Car Parts Stock
item.description: Investors looking for the stock market’s next artificial intelligence winner have honed in on embattled French car parts maker Valeo SE.

item.title: South Africa’s $218 Billion Fund Again Rocked by Governance Woes
item.description: Africa’s biggest fund manager is again tangled up in a governance and management crisis, while its unlisted investments continue to underperform.

item.title: Gilt Traders Set to Give Their Verdict on Burnham Win
Supertankers With 80 Million Barrels of Oil Ready to Pass Hormuz
item.description: Supertankers laden with almost 80 million barrels of oil are sitting in the Persian Gulf and ready to cross the Strait of Hormuz at a moment’s notice, if traders and shipowners give the go-ahead.

item.title: Indonesia Widens Probe to Foundations Tied to Free-Meal Kitchens
item.description: Indonesia expanded its corruption probe into President Prabowo Subianto’s flagship, $15-billion free-meals program into foundations involved in the nationwide rollout of kitchens.

item.title: India Seen Making Slow Return to Mideast Oil as Hormuz Reopens
item.description: India’s state-run refiners have already secured enough crude for the next two months and are in no rush to resume purchases from the Middle East even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens to commercial traffic.

item.title: Indian Software Stocks Tumble After Accenture Warns on Growth
item.description: Shares of Indian software firms tumbled on Friday, joining a global selloff in tech stocks after Accenture Plc forecast slower revenue growth, deepening concerns over a sector that has shed nearly 30% of its market value this year.

item.title: Emerging Equities Touch Record High as Iran Deal Takes Effect
item.description: A gauge of emerging-market equities touched a record high as shipping began to resume in the Strait of Hormuz, paving the way for lower global oil prices.

item.title: Early Users of Anthropic Mythos Still Have Access After US Order
item.description: Some firms chosen early on by Anthropic PBC to test the Mythos AI model ahead of a wider release have preserved their access to a preview of the system, despite a US government order that led to the total shutdown of other versions.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Lops $500 Off Gold Target on No Fed Cuts This Year
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. cut its year-end gold forecast by $500 an ounce as the Federal Reserve is no longer seen reducing rates in 2026.

item.title: Thailand Sees Inflows as Funds Exit Indonesia, Finance Head Says
item.description: Thailand is attracting capital inflows partly at the expense of Indonesia, as investors seek markets with stronger fiscal fundamentals and lower perceived risks, according to its finance chief.

item.title: Russia to Cut Key Rate as Iran War Oil Dividend Eases Inflation
item.description: Russia is poised to lower its key interest rate for a ninth straight meeting as inflation slows, helped in part by knock-on effects from the Middle East turmoil that have worked in the Kremlin’s favor.

item.title: Reliance AGM Puts Jio IPO in Focus After India’s NSE Filing
item.description: The Jio IPO could give Reliance shares a much-needed boost after a long underperformance.

item.title: Burnham Wins UK Seat, Clearing Way for Run at Starmer’s Job
item.description: Andy Burnham won a historic contest for an open UK parliamentary seat, paving the way for the Greater Manchester mayor to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer for his job.

item.title: Currency Traders Pile Into Dollar Call Options After Hawkish Fed
item.description: Currency traders including hedge funds are loading up on options wagers that the dollar will extend gains after the Federal Reserve’s hawkish policy decision this week reinforced expectations for higher US interest rates.

item.title: Australian Beef Exports Face 55% China Tariff as Quota Reached
item.description: Australian beef exports to China will face an additional 55% tariff starting this weekend after hitting Beijing’s annual quota limit, which could impact trade flows and prompt producers to seek new markets for red meat.

item.title: Fujikura Shares Set to Soar as Outlook Hike Calms Market Jitters
item.description: Japanese fiber-optic cablemaker Fujikura Ltd.’s shares are set to surge to their daily limit after the firm unexpectedly raised its profit guidance above market estimates.

item.title: NZ’s Meat Shipments to US Surge, Lifting Total Exports to Monthly Record
item.description: New Zealand exports surged to a second straight monthly record in May, driven by meat shipments to the US and milk consignments, as farmers continue to benefit from strong demand and high commodity prices.

item.title: India Short-Bond Rally Faces Risks From Cash Drain, Analysts Say
item.description: A blazing rally in India’s short-end bonds, driven by plans to attract foreign capital, may fizzle out because the central bank is expected to drain excess cash from the financial system, according to analysts.

item.title: Goldman Says UK’s Sterling Now the Most Overvalued G10 Currency
item.description: The pound is set to face growing pressure as its recovery from the post-Brexit selloff has made it the most overvalued currency among major peers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Katayama Warns of Bold Action With Yen Near 40-Year Low
item.description: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said the government can take bold action against speculative moves, as the yen trades not far from the weakest level in four decades.

item.title: US Tells ASML It’s Concerned China May Have Top Chip Tool
item.description: Dutch chip-equipment giant ASML Holding NV is contending with its biggest challenge yet under the Trump administration: In a series of recent meetings, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick outlined concerns to ASML’s senior leaders that one of its top-of-the-line machines may have made its way into China, in violation of US-led export restrictions.

item.title: US Starts Section 301 Probe of Germany Over Pharmaceuticals
item.description: The US has begun a fresh tariff investigation into Germany over what it called “persistent underpayment for innovative pharmaceutical products,” setting the stage for levies on its goods in retaliation.

item.title: S. Korean Producer Prices Rise Most Since 2022 on Energy Costs
item.description: South Korea’s producer prices rose in May at the fastest pace in nearly four years, as higher energy costs and a broad-based increase in industrial prices continued to filter through the economy.

item.title: BOJ’s Himino Notes Risk of Price Trend Rising Above 2% Target
item.description: There’s a risk that Japan’s price trend could accelerate above the central bank’s 2% inflation target, Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino said as he explained the rationale for the interest rate hike earlier in the week.

item.title: US Nuclear Pilot Program Notches Second Reactor Breakthrough
item.description: Valar Atomics Inc., a Southern California-based startup, has reached a key milestone in its effort to develop small reactors under a US program aimed at accelerating the deployment of nuclear power.

item.title: BHP Shares Fall After $2.3 Billion Writedown on Potash Mine
item.description: BHP Group shares fell after the company flagged a $2.3 billion writedown on its giant potash mine in Canada due to cost and time overruns for an expansion of the project.

item.title: MSCI Flags South Korea Market Hurdles Ahead of Key Review
item.description: South Korea is yet to resolve several key obstacles facing foreign investors, MSCI Inc. said in its latest market accessibility review, a setback for global funds and the government hoping the country’s reform drive could pave the way toward a future upgrade to developed-market status.

item.title: Gold Set for Weekly Loss as Hawkish Fed Outweighs Peace Deal
item.description: Gold was on track for a third weekly loss, as a hawkish Federal Reserve and rate-hike bets outweighed the signing of an interim peace deal between the US and Iran.
